title: Warehouse Operative
description: **About Us** Home to the iconic Instantor® Brand, Sanbra Fyffe is a market leader in the manufacturing, wholesale and distribution of plumbing supplies to the builder’s merchants trade for 90 years. At Instantor, our values aren’t just words on a page — they shape how we work, support each other, and deliver for our customers. We’re looking for someone who shares these values and wants to thrive in a collaborative, customer\-focused environment. **Teamwork:** All departments within the business act as one team to make Sanbra Fyffe the best it can be. **Customer Focus**: We commit to meet and exceed our customer’s expectations, by placing the customer at the centre of all our decisions. **Quality \& Reliability:** We focus on doing things the right way, the first time; delivering on quality, on time and on cost. **Expertise:** We develop and manufacture solutions to provide our customers with maximum value. **Role Purpose** We are now looking to recruit an additional Warehouse Operative where the role will be based out of our Santry depot where you will report to the Warehouse Manager and Operations Manager. The role will involve a full technical induction and training programme to enable the successful candidate to gain experience with the company’s product offering, allowing them to develop and best fulfil their potential. **Key Responsibilities** * Will ensure all sales orders are accurately picked in a timely manner and despatched according to agreed delivery schedules * Will keep stock in a tidy, orderly manner * Will ensure that no stock leaves stores without the proper paperwork and signatures required * Will handle occasional customer queries at the collections counter * Will alert managers to incomplete orders and advise to re\-order stock if not already on order on the system * Will carry out daily ABC stock taking exercises as required * Will use a forklift when required in accordance with standard forklift operating procedures * Will have basic computer skills i.e. ability to use email and master ‘Intact’ \- sales and inventory system * Will be open to variation in job role as required i.e. flexibility essential in line with needs of the company * Must be a team player, a good people person, have good customer service skills **Key Requirements** * Minimum two years warehouse / picking experience * Forklift / Bendy Experience a distinct advantage * Manual Handling Cert * A good level of physical fitness * Counterbalance license and experience preferred if possible. **Rota** Monday to Thursday: 7:00am – 3:45pm Fridays: 7:00am – 2:45pm for two weeks concurrently. Monday to Thursday: 10:00am – 6:45pm Fridays: 10:00am – 5:45pm on every third week **Benefits** · Competitive Salary · Employer Pension Contribution from start date · Employee Assistance Program (for you, your partner/spouse, and dependents 16\+) · Staff Discounts Learn more at Instantor.ie *Please Note – We do not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies at this time.* *At Instantor we are strongly committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all.* Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €30,500\.00\-€32,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee discount * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Santry, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred) Application question(s): * Do you currently hold an active Counterbalance license? * What are your salary expectations? * Please confirm you are available to work the shifts as outlined. Work Location: In person

title: Warehouse Technician
description: **Warehouse Technician** **Location:** Dunboyne, Co. Meath **Contract:** 11\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract **Pay Rate:** €18\.29 per hour \+ 20% Shift Premium (applicable to evening shifts) **Work Arrangement:** Fully On\-Site **About the Role** We are currently recruiting for a **Warehouse Technician** to join a leading manufacturing facility in **Dunboyne** . This role is ideal for candidates with warehouse, production, manufacturing, or logistics experience who enjoy working in a fast\-paced and regulated environment. You will support day\-to\-day warehouse operations, ensuring materials are received, stored, moved, and dispatched safely and efficiently while maintaining high standards of quality and compliance. **Key Responsibilities** * **Receive** , inspect, and process incoming materials and deliveries. * Perform **material handling** , storage, and movement activities throughout the facility. * Complete **picking and kitting** activities to support manufacturing operations. * Load and unload **vehicles and deliveries** safely and efficiently. * Maintain accurate **inventory records** and support stock\-counting activities. * Operate warehouse systems and complete required **documentation** . * Support **shipping and logistics** activities, including outbound shipments. * Ensure compliance with **safety** , **quality** , and **GMP** requirements. * Assist with **continuous improvement** initiatives within the warehouse. * Collaborate with colleagues across multiple departments to support operational goals. **Requirements** * Previous experience in a **warehouse** , **manufacturing** , or **production** environment is advantageous. * Ability to **lift up to 50 pounds (23kg)** and reliable with a positive attitude. **Shift Pattern** This position operates on a rotating **16/5 shift schedule** : * Week 1: **07:00 – 15:00** * Week 2: **15:00 – 23:00** (includes a **20% shift premium** ) * **Fixed Pay Rate 18\.29 \+ 20% Shift premium.** **This opportunity is an 11\-month contract, on\-site in Dunboy, Ireland, paying 18\.29 with a shift pattern.** **To hear more about this position or to discuss your suitability, please apply today!** If you would like to know more about this role, you can reach me at anamarija.bratovic@recruiters.ie You can also apply using the “Apply Now” button below. **GDPR/Privacy Statement:** By applying here you agree to RECRUITERS storing your CV in our secure applicant tracking system (ATS; Bullhorn). As your consultants, we are dedicated to helping you find a new job in your field of expertise and, equally, to keeping your personal information secure at all times. title: Web Specialist
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Clients \& Markets **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** We are seeking a motivated and detail\-oriented web specialist to join our Digital Team within Clients \& Markets. This is an early\-career role, suited to someone looking to build str ong web publishing , front\-end implementation and digital experience skills in a fast\-paced, collaborative environment. As part of a centralised marketing team , you will support the delivery and optimisation of the PwC Ireland website and associated digital channels, working closely with colleagues across design, content and campaigns. You will contribute to the creation of high\-quality, accessible and user\-focused web experiences that support client engagement and position PwC as a leading professional services firm. **Role and responsibilities** * Support the build, publishing and maintenance of web pages using Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) * Work with designers and content specialists to translate content and concepts into functional web experiences * Contribute to ongoing website optimisation, including page performance, accessibility and usability improvements * Support troubleshooting and issue resolution across the website and digital channels * Assist in the creation of simple wireframes and prototypes to support campaign and content development * Apply brand and design standards consistently across all digital outputs * Use analytics and user behaviour data to support continuous improvement of site structure and user journeys * Ensure content and components are optimised for cross\-device and cross\-browser compatibility * Support integrated campaign delivery across web and digital channels * Apply HTML, CSS and JavaScript to support responsive, accessible and performant front\-end implementation, including troubleshooting and customisations **Education / professional qualifications** * A recognised degree (or equivalent) in Web Development, Digital Media, Interactive Design or a related discipline **Experience** * 2 years’ relevant experience, through internships, placements or early career roles * Experience working in a collaborative or project\-based environment is beneficial **Skills and capabilities** * Working knowledge of Content Management Systems; experience with Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) is an advantage * Good understanding of HTML, CSS and core JavaScript concepts * Familiarity with modern front\-end approaches (e.g. modular or component\-based development) is desirable * Awareness of responsive and mobile\-first design principles * Understanding of web accessibility standards and inclusive design principles * Exposure to performance optimisation concepts (e.g. load speed, image optimisation, efficient code practices) * Basic knowledge of analytics tools such as Google Analytics or Adobe Analytics * Familiarity with design and prototyping tools (e.g. Adobe Creative Suite, Figma or similar) * Understanding of SEO fundamentals and how content and structure impact visibility * Awareness of cross\-browser compatibility and testing approaches * Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality **Personal attributes** * Strong team player, comfortable working with a range of stakeholders * Willingness to learn, take feedback and develop technical and professional skills * Practical and proactive approach to problem solving * Clear communicator, able to explain ideas in a structured way * Strong focus on delivering a high\-quality experience for users * Positive, flexible attitude with the ability to manage multiple priorities **Why this role** This role offers the opportunity to build core digital and development skills within a leading professional services firm, working on high\-impact client\-facing platforms. You will gain hands\-on experience across web development, user experience and digital optimisation, supported by a collaborative team environment and structured development opportunities. Our commitment to you We’re committed to building an inclusive culture that empowers our people to thrive and feel a sense of belonging at PwC. We seek to attract talented people from different backgrounds with diverse perspectives who can bring innovation and creativity. At PwC, your hard work doesn't go unnoticed. We celebrate your achievements not just within the firm, but among your peers as well. Our rewards are designed to fit your unique lifestyle and needs . And because being part of PwC should be rewarding every day, you'll have access to PwC perks a handpicked selection of exclusive discounts, experiences, and extras just for being part of our team. Application support We are committed to an inclusive recruitment experience. If you require any adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know ( ie\_experiencedhireteam@pwc.com ). All requests will be treated confidentially and supported wherever possible to help you showcase your strengths. **Education** *(if blank, degree and/or field of study not specified)* Degrees/Field of Study required: Degrees/Field of Study preferred: **Certifications** *(if blank, certifications not specified)* **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Adobe Experience Manager (AEM), Campaign Management, Campaign Performance Analysis, Communication, Communications Management, Communications Strategy, Competitive Analysis, Content Delivery, Content Management, Content Marketing, Content Strategy, Data Analytics, Data\-Driven Insights, Data Visualization, Digital Analytics, Digital Audience Targeting, Digital Business Development, Digital Content Creation, Digital Development, Digital Marketing, Digital Strategies, Email Newsletter Design {\+ 21 more} **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** 0% **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** No **Government Clearance Required?** No **Job Posting End Date**

title: Windows Administrator
description: We are hiring for Role: Windows Administrator Location: Dublin, Ireland Exp: 7 \+ Years Note: Need Local Resource without visa sponsorship Job Description: Key Responsibilities • Hands on administration and maintenance of hosted Windows infrastructure and related services, including proactive identification and resolution of potential incidents. • Take primary responsibility for monthly Windows patching of critical infrastructure, with a particular focus on: o Active Directory domain controllers o Bastion / jump servers deployed as high availability pairs across all regions o Execute patching in a controlled, sequential manner • Perform pre patch and post patch health checks, including: o AD replication and authentication o Core Windows services • Participate in and follow ITIL aligned operational processes including incident, change, problem, and configuration management. • Manage and support large estates of virtual machines running critical customer workloads, including provisioning, lifecycle management, and execution of planned changes. • Perform day\-to\-day infrastructure operations across compute, storage, and virtualisation layers, working within established operational and change management processes. • Respond to infrastructure incidents in a structured and timely manner, working collaboratively to restore service and minimise customer impact. • Work closely with other infrastructure, network, security, and application teams to deliver reliable hosted services aligned with customer and business requirements. **Experience / Qualifications Required** • Strong hands on experience with Windows Server infrastructure in large virtualised hosting or data centre environments where availability is paramount. • Strong Windows skills, particularly Active Directory and Group Policy • Proven experience operating and maintaining production Windows systems in high availability designs • Proven experience in Group policy design and maintenance • Excellent Microsoft security skills • Strong troubleshooting and fault finding skills • Experience working within formal change and operational processes • A careful, methodical approach to operational tasks in availability critical environments • Experience with VMWare or Nutanix **Desirable skills:** • Good client and relationship management ability with excellent oral and written communication and presentation skills • Able to demonstrate flexibility, motivation as well as excellent problem solving and team working • Knowledge of service monitoring and alerting (Nagios, Cacti preferred). • Experience with Nutanix and migration to it from VMWare • Experience of Dell Powerstore or Powerflex storage • PowerShell scripting for automation, validation, or reporting • Familiarity with bastion / jump host security models • ITIL knowledge and practical application • Experience in hosting providers, MSPs, or regulated environments Thanks, Aswini Rajesh
